chapter: ENG9_6 – Weathering the Storm in Ersama
title: Weathering the Storm in Ersama – Medium LAQs

questions.question: Describe the situation in Ersama when the cyclone struck.
questions.answer: When the cyclone struck Ersama, strong winds and heavy rain caused severe destruction. Trees were uprooted, houses collapsed, and floodwaters covered the entire area. People struggled to survive the dangerous conditions.

questions.question: Explain how Prashant and the family managed to survive the cyclone.
questions.answer: Prashant and his friend’s family climbed onto the roof of their house to escape the rising floodwaters. They stayed there for several days and survived by eating tender coconuts from fallen coconut trees.

questions.question: Explain why Prashant decided to return to his village.
questions.answer: Prashant was worried about the safety of his family after the cyclone. As soon as the rain stopped and floodwaters began to recede, he decided to travel back to his village to check whether they were alive.

questions.question: Describe the difficulties faced by Prashant during his journey.
questions.answer: Prashant faced many difficulties such as deep floodwaters, destroyed roads, and floating bodies. Sometimes he had to swim and constantly used a stick to find the road safely.

questions.question: What scenes of destruction did Prashant witness?
questions.answer: Prashant saw villages destroyed, houses washed away, uprooted trees, and floating bodies of humans and animals. These sights showed the terrible impact of the cyclone.

questions.question: Describe Prashant’s feelings when he reached his village.
questions.answer: Prashant felt shocked and heartbroken when he saw the destruction of his village. He feared that his family might not have survived.

questions.question: How did Prashant finally find his family?
questions.answer: Prashant went to the Red Cross shelter where villagers had gathered for safety. There he met his grandmother and other family members who had survived the cyclone.

questions.question: Describe the conditions inside the cyclone shelter.
questions.answer: The shelter was overcrowded with thousands of people who had lost their homes. There was little food and poor sanitation, and many people were injured or grieving.

questions.question: Explain how Prashant arranged food for the villagers.
questions.answer: Prashant organised a group of youths and elders to persuade a merchant to provide rice. Their collective effort finally succeeded and food was arranged.

questions.question: How did Prashant organise the cleaning of the shelter?
questions.answer: He formed a group of youth volunteers who cleaned the shelter and helped injured people to maintain hygiene and prevent disease.

questions.question: Explain the role of helicopters in providing relief.
questions.answer: Helicopters dropped food parcels and supplies for the survivors. This helped provide essential support to the villagers.

questions.question: Describe the idea used by villagers to communicate hunger to helicopters.
questions.answer: Children lay on the sand with utensils on their stomachs to signal hunger. This creative method helped helicopters understand their need for food.

questions.question: What problems did orphaned children face after the cyclone?
questions.answer: Many children lost their parents and homes. They faced emotional trauma and needed care and protection.

questions.question: How did Prashant help orphaned children?
questions.answer: Prashant arranged shelter for orphaned children and ensured that women from the community cared for them.

questions.question: Why did Prashant organise sports activities for children?
questions.answer: Sports helped children overcome grief and emotional trauma. It also brought joy and normalcy to their lives.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the food-for-work programme.
questions.answer: The programme helped women earn food while working, allowing them to rebuild their lives and regain confidence.

questions.question: Why did Prashant oppose sending children and widows to institutions?
questions.answer: He believed that children and widows would feel lonely in institutions and should instead remain within the community.

questions.question: How did Prashant demonstrate leadership during the crisis?
questions.answer: Prashant organised relief work, arranged food, cleaned shelters, and supported vulnerable people, showing strong leadership.

questions.question: Describe the role of women in rebuilding the community.
questions.answer: Women cared for orphaned children and participated in the food-for-work programme, helping rebuild the community.

questions.question: Explain how villagers helped each other after the cyclone.
questions.answer: They shared food, cared for the injured, supported orphaned children, and worked together to rebuild their lives.

questions.question: Why is Prashant admired by his villagers?
questions.answer: He is admired because he helped organise relief work and supported the community selflessly.

questions.question: Explain how the story highlights courage.
questions.answer: Prashant shows courage by travelling through dangerous floodwaters and leading relief efforts to help his community.

questions.question: How does the story show the strength of community spirit?
questions.answer: People worked together, shared resources, and supported vulnerable members, showing strong community spirit.

questions.question: Explain how the villagers slowly rebuilt their lives.
questions.answer: Through cooperation, relief efforts, and programmes like food-for-work, villagers gradually recovered from the disaster.

questions.question: Discuss the main message of 'Weathering the Storm in Ersama'.
questions.answer: The story highlights courage, leadership, and community cooperation in overcoming natural disasters.
